Package javashooter.playground

Class Playground

java.lang.Object
javashooter.playground.Playground
Direct Known Subclasses:
MousePickerLevel
public abstract class Playground extends Object
Playground represents a level of the game, focussing on the game LOGIC and the graphical representation of the level itself (not its GameObject instances). In particular, an instance of Playground
  • manages the different moving or static objects in a level (e.g., collisions, adding objects, removing objects). This is mainly done by the methods addObject(javashooter.gameobjects.GameObject), deleteObject(java.lang.String) and getObject(java.lang.String).
  • processes keyboard inputs provided by GameMain
  • represents the state of a level represented by flags. Each flag has a name (a String) and a value of String, Integer or Double. Methods: setFlagAsInt(java.lang.String, java.lang.Integer, boolean), getFlagAsInt(java.lang.String, boolean, java.lang.Integer). As an example, the current score is a flag usually named "points", with an Integer as a value. This value can be retrieved and manipulated using the above mentioned methods. The reason we use flags and not direct attributes of the playground (or derived) class for communication is that, in this way, the controllers/artist/etc instances do not need to assume that the playground class is of a particular type.

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public abstract String getName()
      Returns the levels static name, to be redefined.

    • addObject

      public void addObject(GameObject o)
      Adds a graphics object to a level. Not immediately but in the next time step to avoid inconsistencies.
      Parameters:
      o - GameObject The object to be added

    • addObjectNow

      public void addObjectNow(GameObject o)
      Adds a graphics object to a level. NOT recommended!
      Parameters:
      o - GameObject The object to be added

    • preferredSizeX

      public abstract int preferredSizeX()
      Here, the level communicates its size preference to the GameUI, called automatically.

    • preferredSizeY

      public abstract int preferredSizeY()

    • broadcast

      public void broadcast(String message)
      Sends a broadcast message to all registered objects, not immediately but in next timestep to avoid inconsistencies.
      Parameters:
      message - The String message to be broadcasted.

    • processBroadcastMessages

      public void processBroadcastMessages()
      Processes all pending broadcast messages and notifies the relevant objects.

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Reinitializes the level.

    • cleanupBroadcasts

      public void cleanupBroadcasts()
      Performs final cleanup operations @ the end of a timestep

    • collectObjects

      public LinkedList<GameObject> collectObjects(String substr, boolean filterInactive)
      Search all game objects for objects with a certain substring in their name. USEFUL!
      Parameters:
      substr - String. The string that must be part of the object name if object is to be considered found.
      filterInactive - deprecated, does nothing regardless of value
      Returns:
      a reference to a LinkedList filled with all objects that have substr in thier name

    • collectObjects

      public LinkedList<GameObject> collectObjects(GameObjectPred p)

    • deleteObject

      public void deleteObject(String id)
      Removes a graphics object from a level, not immediately but in the next timestep.
      Parameters:
      id - String The unique identifier of the object

    • deleteObjectNow

      public void deleteObjectNow(String id)
      Removes a graphics object from a level immediately, NOT recommended.
      Parameters:
      id - String The unique identifier of the object

    • getObject

      public GameObject getObject(String id)
      Retrieves a graphics object by name.
      Parameters:
      id - String Unique id of the object
      Returns:
      reference to the requested game object, or null if not found

    • processKeyEvents

      public void processKeyEvents(Stack<KeyEvent> keyEvents)
      Internal, NEVER call directly.
      Parameters:
      keyEvents - Set of all keyEvents received by the UI in this timestep.

    • processMouseEvents

      public void processMouseEvents(Stack<MouseEvent> mouseEvents)
      Internal, NEVER call directly.
      Parameters:
      mouseEvents - Set of all MouseEvents received by the UI in this timestep.

    • getKeyEvents

      public Stack<KeyEvent> getKeyEvents()
      Can be called by, e.g., ObjectController instances to react to user inputs.
      Returns:
      list of current unhandled key events

    • getMouseEvents

      public Stack<MouseEvent> getMouseEvents()
      Can be called by, e.g., ObjectController instances to react to user inputs.
      Returns:
      list of current unhandled mouse events

      public abstract boolean resetRequested()

      public void updateObjects()
      Calls all object update methods in level. Internal, never call directly.

    • setTimestep

      public void setTimestep(double s)

    • getTimestep

      public double getTimestep()

    • setGameTime

      public void setGameTime(double s)

    • getGameTime

      public double getGameTime()

    • setLevelController

      public void setLevelController(ObjectController ctrl)
      Installs a level javashooter.controller, i.e., a normal ObjectController instance attached to a dummy object that is invisible and has not javashooter.collider. Since every javashooter.controller has access to the Playground instance of its associated object, it can control the Playground instance. For example, applyGameLogic() could be replaced by a dedicated LevelController.

    • updateLevel

      public void updateLevel()

    • redrawLevel

      public abstract void redrawLevel(Graphics2D g2)
      To be redefined!! Draws level background and global information like points etc., NOT moving objects.
      Parameters:
      g2 - Graphics2D abstract drawing object of java Swing, used to carry out all drawing operations.

    • redrawLevelAfter

      public void redrawLevelAfter(Graphics2D g2)
      To be redefined!! Draws mainly h level background and global information like points etc., NOT moving objects but AFTER having drawn objects.
      Parameters:
      g2 - Graphics2D abstract drawing object of java Swing, used to carry out all drawing operations.

    • redraw

      public void redraw(Graphics2D g2)
      Internal, do not call directly.
      Parameters:
      g2 - Graphics2D abstract drawing object of java Swing, used to carry out all drawing operations.

    • prepareLevel

      public abstract void prepareLevel(String level)
      Sets up a single level. Prepares all objects etc. Is called ONCE at the start of each level by the game engine.
      Parameters:
      level - String a string identifying the level number etc

    • setFlagAsInt

      public void setFlagAsInt(String key, Integer value, boolean global)
      Sets a flag with an Integer value.
      Parameters:
      key - The key of the flag.
      value - The Integer value to set.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is not an Integer.

    • setFlagAsString

      public void setFlagAsString(String key, String value, boolean global)
      Sets a flag with a String value.
      Parameters:
      key - The key of the flag.
      value - The String value to set.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is not a String.

    • setFlagAsDouble

      public void setFlagAsDouble(String key, Double value, boolean global)
      Sets a flag with a Double value.
      Parameters:
      key - The key of the flag.
      value - The Double value to set.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is not a Double.

    • getFlagAsInt

      public Integer getFlagAsInt(String key, boolean global, Integer def)
      Retrieves the value of a global flag as an Integer.
      Parameters:
      key - The key of the flag.
      Returns:
      The value of the flag as an Integer, or null if the flag does not exist or is not an Integer.

    • getFlagAsString

      public String getFlagAsString(String key, boolean global, String def)
      Retrieves the value of a global/level-flag as a String.
      Parameters:
      key - The key of the flag.
      Returns:
      The value of the flag as a String, or null if the flag does not exist or is not a String.

    • getFlagAsDouble

      public Double getFlagAsDouble(String key, boolean global, Double def)
      Retrieves the value of a global flag as a Double.
      Parameters:
      key - The key of the flag.
      Returns:
      The value of the flag as a Float, or null if the flag does not exist or is not a Float.

    • incrementFlag

      public void incrementFlag(String key, boolean global, Number def, Number amount)
      Erhöht den Wert eines numerischen Flags (Integer, Double, etc.) um einen bestimmten Betrag.
      Parameters:
      key - Der Schlüssel des Flags.
      amount - Der Betrag, um den der Wert erhöht werden soll (kann positiv oder negativ sein).
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - Wenn der Flag nicht existiert oder kein numerischer Typ ist.
